es.text: Tener la sartén por el mango
es.literal: to hold the pan by the handle
es.meaning: To be in control; to have the upper hand.
es.example: En esta negociación, ellos tienen la sartén por el mango.
es.exampleTranslation: In this negotiation, they hold the upper hand.

it.text: Tenere il coltello dalla parte del manico
it.literal: to hold the knife by the handle end
it.meaning: To be the one in control.
it.example: È lui che tiene il coltello dalla parte del manico.
it.exampleTranslation: He's the one calling the shots.

bridge.note: Both grip the handle for control; mango / manico are near-cognates.

interference.it_to_es: Spanish mango = handle here, NOT the fruit. Classic false-friend trap.

review.notes: Direction of the mango warning assigned it→es on import (the trap bites when reading/producing Spanish); reviewer to confirm.

hook.headline: In Spanish, mango is a handle. Not the fruit.
hook.subhead: Same grip, same idiom — and a fruit lying in wait.
